content: We can frame Self-Refine's iterative self-improvement loop as a foundational single-perspective base to build multi-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a hierarchical self-reflection framework that retains Self-Refine's advantage of no extra training or supervision while resolving its blind spot of consistent internal bias. This framework would first let the LLM generate and iteratively refine an initial output via the standard Self-Refine process, then leverage Self-Contrast or Mirror-style multi-perspective reflection to spot inconsistent flaws that single-perspective self-feedback would miss.
